vulnerability.title: Enable Certmap in SSSD
vulnerability.description: SSSD should be configured to verify the certificate of the user or group. To set this up ensure that section like certmap/testing.test/rule_name is setup in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. For example [certmap/testing.test/rule_name] matchrule =<SAN>.*EDIPI@mil maprule = (userCertificate;binary={cert!bin}) domains = testing.test
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Enable Certmap in SSSD

triage_decision.reason: Enabling certmap in SSSD is a standard STIG hardening measure for certificate-based authentication. It involves a simple configuration edit in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf and follows auth/ssh/sudo/pam change patterns that are safe to remediate, with low risk of lockout or system breakage when applied correctly.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential authentication issues if certmap configuration is incorrect

commands_executed: if [ -f /etc/sssd/sssd.conf ]; then cat /etc/sssd/sssd.conf; else echo "FILE_NOT_FOUND"; fi

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The user wants me to remediate an OpenSCAP finding related to enabling certmap in SSSD on Rocky Linux. The finding is:

- Title: Enable Certmap in SSSD
- Rule Name: sssd_enable_certmap
- Rule ID: xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sssd_enable_certmap
- Finding ID: sssd_enable_certmap
- Severity: 2

The description says: "SSSD should be configured to verify the certificate of the user or group. To set this up ensure that section like certmap/testing.test/rule_name is setup in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. For example [certmap/testing.test/rule_name] matchrule =<SAN>.*EDIPI@mil maprule = (userCertificate;binary={cert!bin}) domains = testing.test"

So I need to add a certmap section to /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. The example shows a section like [certmap/testing.test/rule_name] with specific configuration options.

Let me first read the current /etc/sssd/sssd.conf to see what's already there, then plan the modification.

Steps:
1. Read /etc/sssd/sssd.conf to see current configuration
2. Plan: Add a certmap section with appropriate values. Since this is a remediation, I should follow the example pattern but adapt to the actual environment. The example uses "testing.test" as the domain, but I need to use the actual domain(s) configured in the system.
3. After reading the file, I'll need to determine what domains are configured and add a corresponding certmap section.

Let me start by reading the file.
